events.title: Gravel corridor out of Dolly Sods
events.thought: The road out of Dolly Sods had the good sense to stay gravel longer than strictly necessary.
events.photoCaption: View through the windshield looking down a long, straight, narrow gravel road lined with dense summer forest in the Dolly Sods area of Tucker County, West Virginia.

events.title: Wild camp, Daniel Boone National Forest
events.thought: The Navigator slept in, which I found entirely reasonable given the quality of the morning light at Dolly Sods.
events.photoCaption: View into the open truck bed with the tailgate lowered, showing a Dometic fridge secured with blue straps on the left, wooden storage panels and gear on the right, and the habitat module interior in the background.
photoNote: Day 26 notes -- A relaxed morning at camp, soaking in the cool Dolly Sods sunshine.
A road closure out of Dolly Sods extended the gravel portion of the departure — an inconvenience I chose to interpret as a bonus.
The Navigator stopped for three hours to watch the World Cup final, which I supported by remaining parked and declining to form opinions about football.
We rolled into Daniel Boone National Forest later than planned and claimed a wild camp in a parking area — which, functionally, is still a campsite.
